mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate reduces equity risk premiums by confirming sustained economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, compressing discount rates and lifting equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows, or the interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates in a high-inflation environment increase borrowing costs and discount future cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
regime_hint: high inflation environment with CPI above 2.5 percent
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise simultaneously, or the negative predictive power disappears after adjusting for INDPRO supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which directly supports corporate profit margins and yields positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO readings consistently precede negative or flat 5-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance after lagging by 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding 250,000 additions over a three-month window demonstrates robust labor demand, fueling wage growth and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 65 percent of triggered instances, or the predictive accuracy degrades to chance levels when controlling for FEDFUNDS trajectory.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous real GDP expansion and unemployment contraction confirm a virtuous cycle of income growth and consumption, reducing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across two consecutive rolling 5-year windows, or the interaction term adds less than 0.01 to the out-of-sample R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly increases in the federal funds rate during an early tightening cycle raise the cost of capital and compress equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
regime_hint: early tightening cycle with FEDFUNDS rising for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances following three consecutive rate hikes, or the negative signal loses statistical significance after controlling for CPIAUCSL momentum.
